title: Application of stem cell glycomics for efficient generation of de novo iPS cell lines using glycan biomarkers
author: T. Satomaa
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: All human cells are covered with a glycocalyx made of glycan structures that are characteristic to the cell type. We have studied the glycome of many human stem cell types and differentiated cells with modern glycan analysis methods. Based on this pioneering stem cell glycomics research, we have discovered antibodies against pluripotency markers that specifically recognize glycans on the surface of emerging iPS cells. Automatization with magnetic bead selection and FACS has enabled us to replace the laborious manual phase of cell colony selection and passaging normally associated with iPS cell line generation. Increased efficacy has brought down the cost of iPSC generation so that swift generation of large numbers of iPS cell lines is possible. Further, isolation of the newly formed iPSCs at a time when the colonies are only starting to form considerably shortens the time of the process. The generated iPSC clones are then expanded and characterized with standard methods. We have demonstrated that the technology is robust and applicable to difficult-to-reprogram samples due to the isolation step that can efficiently capture the struggling iPS cells. Either fibroblasts or mononuclear blood cells are reprogrammed using Sendai virus based or episomal and traceless reprogramming vectors. The process is already xeno-free on all the other steps and reagents except for the antibodies used in the sorting, which can be replaced with xeno-free antibodies to form a completely GMP-compatible process. Thus, generation of pure iPS cell lines using glycan biomarkers may enable translation to also clinical applications in the future.   Disclosure : TS, JH and JS are co-founders of Glykos Finland Ltd. that has a business in iPSC generation (Medeina Cell Technologies).
